title: Cisco AI Endpoint Analytics — IoT/OT Classification Confidence and New-Profile Discovery
dataSources: ISE syslog `index=nac sourcetype=cisco:ise:syslog` *AI Endpoint Analytics* category — codes `80100` (AIEA classification), `80101` (AIEA new endpoint type discovered), `80102` (AIEA confidence below threshold), `80103` (AIEA model update). Cisco AI Endpoint Analytics is delivered through Catalyst Center / DNAC and integrates with ISE 3.x. Field: `endpoint_mac`, `endpoint_type`, `confidence_score`, `model_version`.
spl:
index=nac sourcetype="cisco:ise:syslog" MessageCode IN (80100, 80101, 80102)
| stats count, dc(endpoint_mac) as endpoints, avg(confidence_score) as avg_conf by endpoint_type, MessageCode
| where avg_conf < 70 OR MessageCode=="80101"
description: Tracks Cisco AI Endpoint Analytics classification confidence and discoveries of new endpoint types — useful for IoT/OT environments where unknown devices appear and need classification before policy assignment.
value: AI Endpoint Analytics complements traditional Profiler with ML-based classification. Tracking new-type discoveries provides input to policy refinement; tracking low confidence catches systematic gaps.
knownFalsePositives: (a) Initial deployment — many low-confidence classifications during model warm-up.
mitreAttack: N/A (IoT/OT visibility — measures ISE classification accuracy for OT devices, no adversary technique)

compliance.assurance_rationale: Art.21(2)(d) access control — endpoint classification feeds policy.
CM-8 (System Component Inventory) — endpoint discovery feeds inventory.
A.5.9 (Inventory of information and other associated assets).
compliance.controlObjective: Asset-inventory completeness evidence for IoT/OT endpoints classified by ISE.

controlTest.positiveScenario: Connect a never-seen IoT device. AIEA emits 80101 (new type). UC-17.1.66 fires.
controlTest.negativeScenario: Connect a well-known device type. High confidence; no fire.

grandmaExplanation: ISE uses AI to identify what kind of device just connected. We watch when the AI is unsure or when it sees a brand-new type of device, so we can teach it and refine the rules.
